data.question: What is the most expensive cake and its flavor?

prediction.sql: SELECT T1.Id, T1.Flavor FROM goods AS T1 WHERE T1.Food = "Cake" ORDER BY T1.Price DESC LIMIT 1

data.question: What is the cheapest cookie and its flavor?

prediction.sql: SELECT id, flavor FROM goods WHERE food = "Cookie" AND price = (SELECT MIN(price) FROM goods WHERE food = "Cookie");

data.question: Find the ids of goods that have apple flavor.

prediction.sql: Select id From goods where LOWER(flavor) = LOWER('apple')

data.question: What are the ids of goods that cost less than 3 dollars?

prediction.sql: SELECT Id FROM goods WHERE Price < 3

data.question: List the distinct ids of all customers who bought a lemon cake?

prediction.sql: SELECT DISTINCT r.CustomerId FROM items AS i JOIN goods AS g ON i.Item = g.Id JOIN receipts AS r ON i.Receipt = r.ReceiptNumber WHERE g.Flavor = "Lemon" AND g.Food = "Cake"

data.question: For each type of food, tell me how many customers have ever bought it.

prediction.sql: SELECT g.Food, COUNT(DISTINCT r.CustomerId) AS CustomerCount FROM goods g JOIN items i ON g.Id = i.Item JOIN receipts r ON i.Receipt = r.ReceiptNumber GROUP BY g.Food

data.question: Find the id of customers who shopped at the bakery at least 15 times.

prediction.sql: SELECT CustomerId FROM receipts GROUP BY CustomerId HAVING COUNT(*) >= 15

data.question: What is the last name of the customers who shopped at the bakery more than 10 times?

prediction.sql: SELECT c.LastName FROM customers AS c JOIN receipts AS r ON c.Id = r.CustomerId GROUP BY c.Id HAVING COUNT(*) > 10

data.question: How many types of Cake does this bakery sell?

prediction.sql: SELECT COUNT(DISTINCT flavor) FROM goods WHERE LOWER(food) = LOWER('Cake');

data.question: List all the flavors of Croissant available in this bakery.

prediction.sql: Select distinct flavor From goods Where food = "Croissant"

data.question: Give me a list of all the distinct items bought by the customer number 15.

prediction.sql: SELECT DISTINCT i.Item FROM items i JOIN receipts r ON i.Receipt = r.ReceiptNumber WHERE r.CustomerId = 15

data.question: For each type of food, what are the average, maximum and minimum price?

prediction.sql: SELECT food, AVG(price) AS avg_price, MAX(price) AS max_price, MIN(price) AS min_price FROM goods GROUP BY food

data.question: Find the receipt numbers where both Cake and Cookie were bought.

prediction.sql: SELECT i.Receipt FROM items i JOIN goods g ON i.Item = g.Id WHERE g.Food IN ('Cake','Cookie') GROUP BY i.Receipt HAVING COUNT(DISTINCT g.Food) = 2

data.question: Find all the receipt numbers in which customer id 5 purchased Croissant.
What is the receipt number and date of the receipt in which the most expensive item was bought?

prediction.sql: SELECT r.ReceiptNumber, r.Date FROM receipts r JOIN items i ON r.ReceiptNumber = i.Receipt JOIN goods g ON i.Item = g.Id WHERE g.Price = (SELECT MAX(Price) FROM goods)

data.question: What is the item that was bought the least number of times?

prediction.sql: SELECT Item FROM items GROUP BY Item ORDER BY COUNT(*) ASC LIMIT 1

data.question: How many goods are available for each food type?

prediction.sql: SELECT food, COUNT(*) AS count FROM goods GROUP BY food

data.question: What is the average price for each food type?

prediction.sql: SELECT food, AVG(price) AS average_price FROM goods GROUP BY food

data.question: Find flavor of cakes that cost more than 10 dollars.

data.question: Give me the distinct id and price for all goods whose price is below the average of all goods?

data.question: What are the distinct ids of all goods that are cheaper than some goods of type Tart?

data.question: On which date did some customer buy a good that costs more than 15 dollars?

data.question: Find the top three dates when the most goods were sold.
Which customer shopped most often? How many times?

data.question: For each date, return how many distinct customers visited on that day.

data.question: Give me the first name and last name of customers who have bought apple flavor Tart.

prediction.sql:
SELECT DISTINCT c.FirstName, c.LastName
FROM customers c
JOIN receipts r ON c.Id = r.CustomerId
JOIN items i ON r.ReceiptNumber = i.Receipt
JOIN goods g ON i.Item = g.Id
WHERE LOWER(g.Flavor) = LOWER('apple') AND LOWER(g.Food) = LOWER('Tart')

data.question: What are the ids of Cookies whose price is lower than any Croissant?
